steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage does two things: names two specific buildings as examples of a design trend, then explains the principles behind that trend (post-WWII technology emphasis and traditional preservation). The correct answer must describe both parts accurately.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — cites examples, then traces historical origins: The second part is wrong — the passage explains design principles, not historical origins of the trend. ✗
  • Choice B — cites examples, then describes past material uses: The passage does not discuss past material uses; it describes a philosophy of combining technology with tradition. ✗
  • Choice C — cites examples, then argues one aesthetic dominates the other: The passage presents two aesthetics as coexisting, not one winning out. ✗
  • Choice D — cites examples of a design trend, then establishes the principles underlying the trend: Matches both parts — the two buildings are the examples, and the post-WWII technology/tradition balance is the underlying principle. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
